title: Research Published!
body: Our research paper on flexible urban jeepney networks has been published in the Journal of Transport Geography. This started as a class project, but it grew into a real study about commuting, route inefficiencies, and how graph-based thinking can help represent transport problems better. I’m thankful to my co-authors and everyone who helped us reach this point. It feels special to see student work become something that can contribute to a real-world conversation.

title: What if recipes felt more social?
body: Platebook was our take on a social recipe platform where people could discover, save, and interact around food ideas. We built it with Next.js and Django, with features like recipe lists, likes, comments, and a cooking assistant. It was fun seeing a simple idea turn into something that felt like a real product. What feature would make you actually use a recipe app: saving favorites, AI cooking help, or seeing what other people are cooking?
